rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Why do so many people come to Bangalore ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Some come for sight-seeing and others for education.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Quite a number come for work.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Are houses available for all to live in ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: The Government helps in building houses.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: eenee aagli, kudiyokke niiru, tinnokke tarkaari sikkoodu kasta.
rows.english: Even so, it is difficult to get enough water and vegetables.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: nija, ii uuralli vaasa maadoodu tumba himse.
rows.english: True, it is hell to live in this city.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Do you care to come ? we shall go to a movie ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: illa, tarkaari tarokkooskara naanu markettige hoogbeeku.
rows.english: Sorry, I have to go to the market to buy vegetables.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: nadiiri, haage tindi tinnokke hootlige hoogoona
rows.english: Come on then, let's go to a restaurant for snacks.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: tindi tinnokkoskara hootlige yaake ? ramaan noodokke avla maneg hoogoona.
rows.english: Why go to a hotel for snacks ? Let's go visiting to Rama's house.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: What good would it do ? I'm hungry.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: alli kudiyookke tanniiru, tinnokke aiskriimu, ella sikkutte.
rows.english: We will get clooled water and ice-cream there.
